gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (7/371)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (7/371)
text: قِيلَ لِلرِّضَا ( عليه السلام ) إِنَّكَ تَتَكَلَّمُ بِهَذَا الْكَلَامِ وَ السَّيْفُ يَقْطُرُ دَماً فَقَالَ إِنَّ لِلَّهِ وَادِياً مِنْ ذَهَبٍ حَمَاهُ بِأَضْعَفِ خَلْقِهِ النَّمْلِ فَلَوْ رَامَهُ الْبَخَاتِيُّ لَمْ تَصِلْ إِلَيْهِ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from Muhammad Bin Isa, from Yunus, from the one who mentioned it who said,

‘It was said to Al-Reza<sup>asws</sup>, ‘You<sup>asws</sup> are speaking with this speech and the sword is dripping blood?’ So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Allah<sup>azwj</sup> has a valley of gold, protecting it by the weakest of His<sup>azwj</sup> creatures, the ants. So even if it is sought by the Bukhaty (Persian camels) they would not arrive to it’.
en.sarwar: 11. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from Muhammad ibn ‘Isa from Yunus from the one whom he has mentioned, he has said the following: “It was said to al-Rida (a.s.) ‘You speak of it (Your Divine Authority) and blood drips from the sword (of your enemies).’ The Imam said, ‘Allah has a valley that is gold. He has guarded it with ants, the weakest creature. Even if the camels of Khurasan (North East parts of Iran) try to go there they cannot reach it.’”
